Introduction to Oldham County

Arrows in Oldham County

Arrow #22: Old Tascosa-Boys Ranch

More Details for Arrow #22

Coordinates: 35.531856, -102.260819

Installed: January 26, 2012

Marker Text:

1786-1874 Comanches met Comancheros at Atascosa Creek to feast, gamble, race horses & exchange trade goods

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #76: Vega

More Details for Arrow #76

Coordinates: 35.2460258, -102.4280873

Installed: August 5, 2014

Marker Text:

Comanches camped at playa lakes here, traveling trail N to Tascosa & E/W to Tecovas Springs & Tucumcari

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Map of Arrows in Oldham County

Explore the map below for arrow locations and the county boundary.

Welcome to the Quanah Parker Trail!

Explore the historical arrow markers commemorating Quanah Parker and the Comanche people across the Texas Plains Trail Region. Use the map on the homepage or navigate via the menu to learn more about this effort.
